 {"id":"frankenpandas-d4ht","title":"Add dataframe head/tail saturating negative-n strict conformance packets","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T00:28:04.982027231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T00:32:11.427446798Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T00:32:11.425690144Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-d6xa","title":"[CRITICAL] conformance: 89 live_oracle_* tests silently skip in CI — core pandas-parity claim is not enforced","description":"Applying /testing-conformance-harnesses skill's Pattern 1 (Differential Testing) + the "Before claiming conformance, prove it" rule.

**Observation.** 89 live_oracle_*_matches_pandas tests exist in crates/fp-conformance/src/lib.rs. Each one dispatches through capture_live_oracle_expected → subprocess pandas_oracle.py. The default HarnessConfig sets:

  oracle_root: repo_root.join("legacy_pandas_code/pandas")
  allow_system_pandas_fallback: false

When oracle_root.exists() is false, capture_live_oracle_expected returns HarnessError::OracleUnavailable. The 89 tests match on that variant and early-return with an eprintln!("live pandas unavailable; skipping ..."). No test fails. No test is marked ignored. No summary counter is emitted.

**The repo intentionally gitignores legacy_pandas_code/** (confirmed: .gitignore line "legacy_pandas_code/"). .github/workflows/ci.yml does NOT install pandas, does NOT set FP_PYTHON_BIN, does NOT checkout a pandas oracle source tree, does NOT set allow_system_pandas_fallback=true.

**Consequence.** In CI as shipped today, all 89 live_oracle_* tests silently pass by skipping. The entire differential conformance layer — the exact thing the README markets as "differential conformance tests verify against the actual pandas oracle" — is a no-op in CI. The only thing CI actually verifies is the 1249 pre-generated packet fixtures (FixtureExpected mode, golden-file comparison), which themselves were generated by some earlier pandas version that is not pinned anywhere (br-frankenpandas-8irq covers the version pin separately).

**Severity.** CRITICAL — the core product claim (pandas parity, verified by oracle) is not actually enforced by CI. Any regression that only the live oracle would catch (e.g. a new pandas point release changing semantics we match; a Rust-side bug that golden fixtures predate) lands on main silently.

**Fix shape:**
1. Count-and-fail: HarnessConfig gains a require_live_oracle: bool, default false. When true, OracleUnavailable converts to test failure instead of early-return. ci.yml sets the env var that flips this on.
2. CI installs pandas: ci.yml adds a Python setup step + pip install pandas==PINNED_VERSION (coordinates with br-frankenpandas-8irq). Then clones / mirrors legacy_pandas_code or points oracle_root at the pip-installed pandas via allow_system_pandas_fallback=true.
3. Skip counter: LiveOracleReport aggregates per-test skipped/run/failed counts, printed at end of lib-test run. CI asserts skipped_count == 0 or an explicit allowlist.
4. README update: add "When live pandas is unavailable, these 89 tests skip silently — not a gap in the oracle, a gap in the env" disclosure until (1)-(3) land.

Blast radius: ci.yml (+ ~12 lines), fp-conformance HarnessConfig + tests (+ ~60 lines for the counter + flag). No code-logic change.

Parallel bead: br-frankenpandas-8irq (pin pandas version) becomes a prerequisite for this bead — landing live-oracle-in-CI without a pinned pandas causes CI flakiness on any pandas update.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:10:40.629610903Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T19:33:23.420751862Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T19:33:23.418908373Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","conformance","critical","oracle","review"]}
-{"id":"frankenpandas-d7lt","title":"[MEDIUM] fuzz: DataFrame constructor + merge pub fns lack fuzz targets","description":"/testing-fuzzing coverage audit: DataFrame constructor + merge fuzz targets missing.

**Gap analysis.** Per /testing-fuzzing Rule 3 (every pub fn accepting untrusted input must have a fuzz target). Current coverage:

- fuzz_dataframe_eval — DataFrame.eval (br existing)
- fuzz_pivot_table — pivot_table (br existing)
- fuzz_groupby_sum, fuzz_groupby_agg_dispatch — groupby partials (br existing)
- fuzz_join_series — series-level joins (br existing)
- fuzz_dataframe_op_chain — stateful op sequences (br-wr9n)
- fuzz_parallel_dataframe — concurrency (br-bahw)

**Uncovered pub fns (all take untrusted Vec<Scalar> or &DataFrame):**

1. DataFrame::from_dict(column_order, data)                — 14843
2. DataFrame::from_dict_mixed(...)                         — 15121
3. DataFrame::from_dict_with_index(...)                    — 15425
4. DataFrame::from_dict_with_index_mixed(...)              — 15451
5. DataFrame::from_records(records, column_order, labels)  — 15037
6. DataFrame::from_tuples(records, columns)                — 15055
7. DataFrame::from_series(series_list)                     — 14786
8. merge_dataframes(left, right, on, join_type)            — fp-join 1102
9. merge_dataframes_on(left, right, on, join_type)         — fp-join 1092
10. merge_dataframes_on_with(left, right, left_on, right_on, join_type) — fp-join 817

Each is a canonical fuzz Rule 3 target: user-supplied data (potentially crafted) flows in, frame constructed / joined, results must preserve shape invariants.

**Skill rule invoked:** Rule 3 (fuzz every untrusted-input pub fn).

**Severity.** MEDIUM — same severity as the jkhg (expr) and gpxk (SQL) slices already shipped this session. No known bug, but a structural hole in fuzz coverage of the most-used public API.

**Fix shape (four new targets this slice):**

1. fuzz_dataframe_from_dict_bytes — bytes projected into a tiny (name→values) dict; call DataFrame::from_dict; assert ok(df) has row count == inferred n.
2. fuzz_dataframe_from_records_bytes — bytes projected into a rectangular Vec<Vec<Scalar>>; call DataFrame::from_records; assert rows == records.len.
3. fuzz_dataframe_from_series_bytes — bytes projected into Vec<Series> with shared index; call DataFrame::from_series; assert cols == series.len.
4. fuzz_dataframe_merge_bytes — two small frames with a shared 'k' column; call merge_dataframes(..., JoinType::{Inner,Left,Right,Outer}); assert per-join-type row-count bound (inner <= min; left >= left.rows for unique keys; outer >= max).

Each:
- pub fn fuzz_*_bytes in fp-conformance/src/lib.rs with size guard (≤ 64 KB).
- fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_*.rs wrapper.
- [[bin]] entry in fuzz/Cargo.toml.
- fuzz/corpus/<target>/ with 4 seeds.
- fuzz/artifacts/<target>/README.md.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:27:55.157228356Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:22:30Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["constructors","fuzz","merge","pub-api","review"],"closed_at":"2026-04-24T00:22:30Z","close_reason":"Completed merge fuzz invariant hardening and clippy cleanup"}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-ddox","title":"[HIGH] docs: doctest never run in CI — 8 existing doctests silently unverified + docs.rs will fail at release","description":"Doctest coverage audit.

**Observation.**
- grep for `/// \`\`\`rust` / `/// \`\`\`` / `/// \`\`\`no_run` / `/// \`\`\`ignore` across crates/*/src/lib.rs finds only **8 doctests total** — 2 in frankenpandas, 4 in fp-index, 2 in fp-columnar. The 10 other crates have zero doctests.
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs `cargo test --workspace` at line 32 but does NOT run `cargo test --doc --workspace`. By default `cargo test --workspace` runs `#[test]` fns and integration tests but NOT doctests (those only run with `--doc` or when the default test target is configured to include them).
- Independent confirmation: I grepped for "doc" in ci.yml — returns only the cargo-llvm-cov-related word "Documentation" as false positive, no `--doc` invocation.

**Consequence.**
- The 8 existing doctests never execute in CI. A code example in a public README-level function that compiles today can be silently broken by an API change.
- More importantly, doctests are how Rust libraries document their API contracts with runnable examples. The public-API-contract bead (br-7cfm) called for documenting `# Panics:` on 1530 pub fns; good doctest coverage is the complementary half — runnable examples that VERIFY the documented behavior.
- docs.rs builds doctests when hosting crate docs. Once the release workflow lands (br-4clx) and docs.rs starts serving frankenpandas, any broken doctest fails the docs build → docs.rs shows "docs build failed" instead of the actual API reference → users land on a broken docs page from crates.io.

**Severity.** HIGH — compounds with br-7cfm (panic contract) and br-4clx (release). Without doctest coverage, the public API's "how do I use this?" examples rot silently until a docs.rs build fails publicly at release time.

**Fix shape:**

1. ci.yml adds `cargo test --doc --workspace` as a distinct step after the main `cargo test --workspace`. Catches doctest failures on every PR.
2. Add doctests to the existing pub fn docs systematically:
   - Priority 1: DataFrame constructors (from_dict, new, from_series, from_records) — most-used entry points.
   - Priority 2: Series arithmetic (add, sub, mul, div) — high visibility in README examples.
   - Priority 3: GroupBy core (sum, mean, agg_named, rolling) — conformance-heavy surfaces where doctest doubles as conformance sample.
   - Priority 4-N: Remaining public fns by frequency of use (informed by br-zk1j API matrix once that lands).

   Target: lift from 8 doctests to ~150 over several passes. Each passing doctest also serves as a user-facing example, reducing the pure `# Panics:` prose burden from br-7cfm.

3. `cargo test --doc --doc-args="..."`: for slow doctests, use `rust ignore` or `rust no_run` to bail out of execution while keeping the example visible on docs.rs.
4. Doctest regression: add a `scripts/doctest_count.sh` that fails CI if the doctest count shrinks between commits. Prevents silent removal of examples.

Blast radius: ci.yml (+1 step), ~150 lines of doctest additions (parallelizable across agents, one pub fn at a time), 1 small script. No source logic changes.

Related:
- br-7cfm (panic contract) — doctests verify the panic contract in addition to documenting it.
- br-4clx (release workflow) — doctests build on docs.rs at release; adding them now means no surprise at release time.
- br-zk1j (API matrix) — the matrix can mark "has doctest: yes/no" as an extra coverage column.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:39:57.255680319Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:33:23.551068425Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T16:33:23.548527729Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","docs","doctest","review"]}

-{"id":"frankenpandas-fd90","title":"[EPIC] SQL backend abstraction — trait + PostgreSQL + MySQL","description":"Pandas-parity SQL IO across multiple backends. Today fp_io::read_sql / write_sql hardcode rusqlite::Connection; pandas read_sql/to_sql works with any SQLAlchemy-compatible engine (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, MS SQL, Oracle, ...). Multi-slice umbrella; file child beads per slice.

1. pub trait SqlConnection in fp-io (DBAPI2-like surface): execute_query(sql, params) -> RowIter, execute_statement(sql, params) -> usize, with_transaction<F>(f) -> Result<T>. Minimum surface to cover rusqlite, tokio-postgres, and mysql_async concrete backends behind the same trait.

2. Cargo features: sql-sqlite (default), sql-postgresql, sql-mysql. Each feature brings its backend crate and a wrapper impl SqlConnection for that backend's Connection type. Additive; disabling sql-sqlite drops the rusqlite dep.

3. read_sql / write_sql / read_sql_table / read_sql_query signatures move from rusqlite::Connection to <C: SqlConnection>. Existing SQLite call sites keep working because rusqlite::Connection gets the wrapper impl.

4. Param binding: closes frankenpandas-tk3k by letting the public surface accept params: &[&(dyn ToSqlBindable)] routed through each backend's type-specific binder.

5. chunksize streaming: read_sql(.., chunksize=Some(n)) returns impl Iterator<Item=Result<DataFrame, ...>> to match pandas.

6. coerce_float: read_sql(.., coerce_float=true) promotes NUMERIC / DECIMAL / MONEY to Float64 at row-materialization time.

7. Live-oracle conformance: packet fixtures per backend (sqlite/postgres/mysql) verifying round-trip parity with pandas' pd.read_sql(engine) for each.

Blocker for m3e8 closure: the README now honestly says 'SQL: SQLite-only via rusqlite; PostgreSQL/MySQL planned' (commit-pending, br-m3e8). Closing that qualifier requires this epic landing — at minimum slices (1)-(3).

Decomposition strategy: (1) is the keystone — pick between (a) a minimal DBAPI2-style trait sufficient to host all three backends without leaking backend-specific types into the public API, vs (b) a typestate / erased-impl approach. (2) feature-gates each backend behind Cargo features to keep binary size bounded. (4)-(7) then unblock incrementally behind (1)+(2)+(3).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:02:41.189776401Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-24T01:11:57.085464760Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T01:11:57.083640746Z","close_reason":"Completed minimum SQL backend abstraction slice: generic SqlConnection APIs are in place, rusqlite is gated behind default sql-sqlite, and fp-io builds without default SQL backend features","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["backend-abstraction","epic","io","sql"]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-fd90","title":"[EPIC] SQL backend abstraction — trait + PostgreSQL + MySQL","description":"Pandas-parity SQL IO across multiple backends. Today fp_io::read_sql / write_sql hardcode rusqlite::Connection; pandas read_sql/to_sql works with any SQLAlchemy-compatible engine (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, MS SQL, Oracle, ...). Multi-slice umbrella; file child beads per slice.

1. pub trait SqlConnection in fp-io (DBAPI2-like surface): execute_query(sql, params) -> RowIter, execute_statement(sql, params) -> usize, with_transaction<F>(f) -> Result<T>. Minimum surface to cover rusqlite, tokio-postgres, and mysql_async concrete backends behind the same trait.

2. Cargo features: sql-sqlite (default), sql-postgresql, sql-mysql. Each feature brings its backend crate and a wrapper impl SqlConnection for that backend's Connection type. Additive; disabling sql-sqlite drops the rusqlite dep.

3. read_sql / write_sql / read_sql_table / read_sql_query signatures move from rusqlite::Connection to <C: SqlConnection>. Existing SQLite call sites keep working because rusqlite::Connection gets the wrapper impl.

4. Param binding: closes frankenpandas-tk3k by letting the public surface accept params: &[&(dyn ToSqlBindable)] routed through each backend's type-specific binder.

5. chunksize streaming: read_sql(.., chunksize=Some(n)) returns impl Iterator<Item=Result<DataFrame, ...>> to match pandas.

6. coerce_float: read_sql(.., coerce_float=true) promotes NUMERIC / DECIMAL / MONEY to Float64 at row-materialization time.

7. Live-oracle conformance: packet fixtures per backend (sqlite/postgres/mysql) verifying round-trip parity with pandas' pd.read_sql(engine) for each.

Blocker for m3e8 closure: the README now honestly says 'SQL: SQLite-only via rusqlite; PostgreSQL/MySQL planned' (commit-pending, br-m3e8). Closing that qualifier requires this epic landing — at minimum slices (1)-(3).

Decomposition strategy: (1) is the keystone — pick between (a) a minimal DBAPI2-style trait sufficient to host all three backends without leaking backend-specific types into the public API, vs (b) a typestate / erased-impl approach. (2) feature-gates each backend behind Cargo features to keep binary size bounded. (4)-(7) then unblock incrementally behind (1)+(2)+(3).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:02:41.189776401Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:21:21.081484312Z","close_reason":"Completed merge fuzz invariant hardening and clippy cleanup","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["backend-abstraction","epic","io","sql"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-ffhs","title":"[HIGH] ci: monolithic 'checks' job lumps fmt/lint/test/conformance/gates — per-PR triage slow + parallelism wasted","description":"CI job-splitting / triage-velocity audit.

**Observation.** `.github/workflows/ci.yml` today has TWO jobs:
  - `checks`: monolithic — runs fmt, cargo check, clippy, workspace test, conformance tests, bench smoke, phase2c gate, governance gate. Each failure surfaces as "checks failed."
  - `fuzz-regression`: new, added recently by br-zjme landing — focused on fuzz corpus regression.

(Note: `checks` also installs Python + pandas as of the br-d6xa fix landing — good.)

**Problem with monolithic checks.**

- Median time for a "checks" run (per recent CI artifacts the ~175s test step + ~60-90s clippy + ~80s install-llvm-cov dead step [br-0a83] + smaller steps) ≈ 5-8 minutes.
- When a single step fails — say, `cargo fmt --check` at step 2 of 15 — every subsequent step is skipped, but the full 5-8 min matrix-slot is consumed because GH Actions doesn't parallelize within a job.
- PR reviewers see ONE red "checks" badge and must expand the logs to find which step failed. If multiple PRs are in flight, triage is serial.
- Retrying a fmt failure re-runs all 15 steps. A minor formatting fix wastes 5-8 min of runner time.
- Caching is per-job. Since `checks` does everything, it sees one cache key for 15 disparate workloads; `clippy` and `test` can't benefit from each other's cache separately.

**How the industry solves it (pattern used by tokio, arrow, polars, serde, etc.):**

```yaml
jobs:
  fmt:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps: [ ..., cargo fmt --check ]

  lint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps: [ ..., cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings ]

  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps: [ ..., cargo test --workspace --all-targets ]

  conformance:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [test]   # conformance requires a green test first
    steps: [ install-pandas, cargo test -p fp-conformance --lib ]

  gates:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [conformance]
    steps: [ ./scripts/phase2c_gate_check.sh, ./scripts/governance_gate_check.sh ]

  fuzz-regression:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest  (already exists)
```

Splits produce:
1. Five PARALLEL jobs (fmt / lint / test / conformance / gates / fuzz-regression) — runner total time drops from ~7 min serial to ~4 min parallel (the longest job is the bound).
2. Per-job status in the PR UI: reviewers see 5 named badges, spot the red one immediately.
3. Retry cost: fmt-only retry reruns fmt only (20-30s instead of 5-8 min).
4. Per-job cache keys: lint + test caches separately; no cross-contamination.

**Severity.** HIGH — impacts every single PR in perpetuity. With the swarm's current velocity (20+ commits/day), 5-8 min of CI serialization per PR adds up to hours-per-week of wall-clock friction that splits would eliminate. Also: the monolithic job blocks future additions (br-0l5r cross-platform matrix, br-kmbc feature-matrix) because adding them to `checks` would push it to ~15 min; separate jobs let each matrix ship independently.

**Fix shape:**

1. ci.yml refactor:
   - Split `checks` into fmt / lint / test / conformance / gates (~50 line reorg, per above).
   - Fuzz-regression job stays as-is.
   - Add `needs:` dependencies so conformance runs AFTER test (no sense running conformance if basic tests fail).

2. Optional enhancement (can ship same PR or follow-up):
   - Add `strategy.matrix` to test job for OS cross-platform (br-0l5r integrates naturally here).
   - Add `strategy.matrix` to test job for feature combos (br-kmbc).

3. Keep llvm-cov install (br-0a83) in its own coverage job or remove the install line until coverage is actually wired.

4. README / CONTRIBUTING (br-6d5s) gets a brief "CI jobs" section listing each job's responsibility.

Blast radius: ci.yml only (+~50 lines refactor, -~15 lines from the existing checks mega-job). Zero source-logic changes. No new dependencies.

Prerequisites: None. Can land independently.

**Related:**
- br-0l5r (cross-platform matrix) — integrates into the split test job.
- br-kmbc (feature matrix) — integrates into the split test job.

 {"id":"frankenpandas-lvl6","title":"[HIGH] fuzz: no crash-to-regression-test infrastructure — Rule 10 violation; any found crash will re-regress","description":"Applying /testing-fuzzing skill Rule 10: "Every crash artifact becomes a regression test or it WILL regress. Untested fixes are temporary fixes."

**Observation.** grep for `fn regression_crash_` / `include_bytes!.*crash-` / `include_bytes!.*artifacts` across the entire workspace returns ZERO hits. No `#[test]` fn in any crate replays a fuzz crash artifact against the fixed parser.

Cross-check: the fuzz_*_io_bytes unit tests in fp-conformance/src/lib.rs are POSITIVE seeds (e.g. `fuzz_csv_parse_bytes_accepts_simple_seed_fixture`) — they test that the fuzz harness correctly processes a valid input. They do NOT replay a historical crash.

**Skill rules involved:**
- Rule 10 (direct): every crash becomes a regression test.
- Triage Pipeline step 8: "REGRESSION → Convert to permanent unit test" with an explicit template:
      #[test]
      fn regression_crash_abc123() {
          let input = include_bytes!("../fuzz/artifacts/my_target/crash-abc123");
          let _ = my_crate::parse(input);  // Must not panic after fix
      }

**What this means today.** Zero crashes have ever been locked in as regression tests. Two possibilities:
- (a) Zero crashes have ever been found. Plausible for a young fuzz corpus with no CI gate (br-zjme).
- (b) Crashes have been found and patched but not regressed. Higher-risk assumption.

Either way, the INFRASTRUCTURE for crash → regression is missing:
- No `fuzz/regressions/` directory to store minimized artifacts as per-target permanent seeds.
- No `tests/fuzz_regressions.rs` module with the template-pattern replays.
- No documentation anywhere on the crash triage flow.

**Consequence.** When br-zjme ships fuzz CI, the first real crash will be fixed by a `fix: parser should reject malformed X` commit. Without this bead's infrastructure, the fix is a one-shot patch. Months later, a refactor reintroduces the bug; no test fails. The fuzzer rediscovers it — or doesn't, if the mutation path that originally found it has drifted out of coverage reach.

**Severity.** HIGH — Rule 10 is one of the skill's "Hard Rules (Non-Negotiable)." We don't have the pattern in place, so every crash we ever find can regress. This is force-multiplier for every other fuzz bead in the backlog.

**Fix shape:**

1. `fuzz/regressions/` directory + `fuzz/regressions/README.md`:
   ```
   Minimized crash artifacts that must always reproduce the pre-fix crash
   (and always pass after the fix). One subdirectory per fuzz target.
   ```

2. `crates/fp-conformance/tests/fuzz_regressions.rs` — new integration test file. Pattern per artifact:
   ```rust
   #[test]
   fn regression_csv_parse_unterminated_quote_2026_05_01() {
       let input = include_bytes!(
           "../../../fuzz/regressions/fuzz_csv_parse/unterminated_quote_2026_05_01.csv"
       );
       // Must not panic; may return Err; semantic must match the fix-commit's intent.
       let _ = fp_io::read_csv_str(std::str::from_utf8(input).unwrap_or_default());
   }
   ```

3. Naming convention:
   - Filename: `{target}/{short_description}_{YYYY_MM_DD}.{ext}`
   - Test fn: `regression_{target}_{short_description}_{YYYY_MM_DD}`
   - Commit message: `fix(fp-io): reject malformed X (br-XXXX)\n\nAdds fuzz regression fuzz_csv_parse/unterminated_quote_2026_05_01.csv.`

4. Pre-commit guard (extends existing 50-agent-mail.py hook):
   - If a commit adds a file under `fuzz/regressions/` but not a matching `#[test]` in `fuzz_regressions.rs`, reject.
   - If a commit adds a `fix(fuzz): ...` commit message but no file under `fuzz/regressions/`, warn.

5. Triage workflow doc in AGENTS.md (the section br-6d5s's CONTRIBUTING.md mirrors):
   - Find crash → `cargo fuzz tmin` → save under regressions/ → add regression test → fix bug → verify test passes.

6. CI gate: `cargo test -p fp-conformance --test fuzz_regressions` runs on every PR. Fast (include_bytes! + one parse call per regression = <1s for hundreds of regressions).

Blast radius:
- 1 new directory (empty initially, grows over time).
- 1 new Rust test file (~20 lines + template).
- 1 new README.
- 1 pre-commit hook check (~20 lines of Python extending the existing hook).
- AGENTS.md section (~30 lines).
- CI change: `cargo test` already includes the new test file via `--all-targets`, so no CI.yml change needed.

**Related:**
- br-zjme (fuzz CI + corpus) — prerequisite for crashes to exist.
- br-i9rj (cmin/tmin) — the minimization step that produces the regression artifact.
- br-auys (memory/time caps) — regression tests for "this input used to OOM" need the same caps to avoid re-OOMing in CI.
- br-7cfm (panic contract) — regression tests are concrete examples that the panic contract says what the code does.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:58:58.967787503Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:28:14.319513311Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T16:28:14.317032960Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","regression","review","workflow"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-lwu8","title":"pandas: DataFrame.apply('prod', axis=0) column-wise parity","description":"py signature: df.apply('prod', axis=0) -> Series

-{"id":"frankenpandas-lxhr","title":"[HIGH] maintenance: fp-frame 63k LOC monolith / fp-conformance 32k LOC monolith block parallel-agent workflow","description":"Maintenance-hazard audit on single-source-file sizes.

**Numbers (fresh wc -l):**
- crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:         63,391 lines / 2.1 MB
- crates/fp-conformance/src/lib.rs:   32,169 lines / 1.2 MB
- crates/fp-io/src/lib.rs:              7,507 lines / 265 KB

**Observation.** `crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs` is 63k+ lines in a single flat .rs file — no `mod` decomposition (no `src/series.rs`, `src/dataframe.rs`, `src/groupby.rs`, `src/rolling.rs`, etc.). The module hosts: Series, DataFrame, Column, Rolling, Expanding, Ewm, Resample, DataFrameRolling, DataFrameResample, DataFrameExpanding, SeriesGroupBy, DataFrameGroupBy, GroupByRolling, GroupByResample, plus all tests and proptests inlined.

**Consequences on the active multi-agent workflow:**
1. Every slice I committed today (1zzp.1 through 1zzp.5) edits fp-frame/src/lib.rs. Every parallel-agent commit the same session edits fp-frame/src/lib.rs. Rebase conflicts are constant — I hit two this session (row_multiindex field + group_keys_as_row_multiindex helper) where I had to re-read the file between edits.
2. Incremental `cargo check -p fp-frame` invalidates the whole 2.1 MB file on any edit. On rch, rebuild time is 20-60 s per iteration purely because of file-scope invalidation, not because the change is semantically large.
3. IDE "jump to definition" / "find references" has to parse the 2.1 MB file for every query. rust-analyzer spends ~2-3x longer than comparable-sized workspaces.
4. PR diff review against this file is unusable — GitHub's diff UI drops into "load diff" prompts; 15k-line PRs flatten into noise.
5. `git blame` for any line requires loading the entire file history; forks of history intersect heavily.

**Same problem, smaller scale, in fp-conformance/src/lib.rs (32k lines)** — holds the fixture runner, 89 live_oracle_* tests, the bootstrap_cases harness, plus all fuzz_*_bytes harnesses (4 of them inlined: 5431-5579 alone is fuzz_dataframe_eval + fuzz_semantic_eq).

**Severity.** HIGH — this is the single largest drag on parallel-agent velocity this session. The issue is structural, not cosmetic: every future slice of any epic (row-MultiIndex was just one of several planned) will compound the conflict surface.

**Fix shape:**

fp-frame split (mandatory order; each step compiles green):
1. `src/column.rs` — Column + ValidityMask + Column::from_values (lives at lib.rs:~3000-4000).
2. `src/series/`        mod with mod.rs + arith.rs + rolling.rs + expanding.rs + ewm.rs (Series-impl blocks currently at lib.rs:~993-6000).
3. `src/dataframe/`     mod with mod.rs + constructors.rs + io_ext.rs + arith.rs + apply.rs + indexing.rs + reshape.rs (DataFrame impl blocks at ~14120-16000).
4. `src/groupby/`       mod with mod.rs + series_groupby.rs + dataframe_groupby.rs + rolling.rs + resample.rs (at ~25782-28074 plus SeriesGroupBy blocks earlier).
5. `src/rolling.rs` + `src/resample.rs` (shared Rolling/Resample builders).
6. Tests move to `src/tests/` mod or co-located `#[cfg(test)]` mod inside each submodule.

fp-conformance split (parallel-safe):
1. `src/harness/` mod: fixture loader, comparator (compare_series_expected / compare_dataframe_expected), oracle dispatch.
2. `src/fuzz/` mod: all fuzz_*_bytes harnesses (4+ of them, which is where br-frankenpandas-liai [diff fuzz target] will land naturally).
3. `src/tests/live_oracle.rs` — the 89 live_oracle_*_matches_pandas tests.
4. `src/tests/packet_suite.rs` — run_packet_suite + run_e2e_suite.

Blast radius: large but mechanical. Split produces structural improvement, no behavior change. Each sub-bead is a separate PR (file by file) so reviews stay tractable.

Prerequisites: none. But should land BEFORE any further epic decomposition (e.g. the fd90 SQL backend epic's 7 slices would otherwise pile onto fp-io as the next monolith).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:58:51.801544673Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-24T01:54:25.572983662Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T01:54:25.571111437Z","close_reason":"Split fp-conformance fuzz entrypoints into a dedicated module while preserving public re-exports","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["maintenance","modularity","review","workflow"]}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-lz1e","title":"[LOW] license: 12 crates all point license-file at ../../LICENSE — publish-time audit tooling may false-positive","description":"Per-crate LICENSE distribution audit.

**Observation.** All 12 crates carry `license-file = "../../LICENSE"` in their Cargo.toml — a relative path that walks OUT of the crate directory. No per-crate `LICENSE` file or symlink exists anywhere under `crates/`.

**What happens on `cargo publish`.** Cargo's publish step builds a `.crate` tarball containing ONLY files inside the crate directory. A `license-file` pointing at `../../LICENSE` is problematic:
- **Pre-cargo-1.77**: `cargo publish` warned on this pattern (VCS check + license-file copy had inconsistent behavior). Published `.crate` tarballs might or might not include the LICENSE file depending on cargo version used at publish time.
- **Post-cargo-1.77**: Cargo copies the license-file INTO the package automatically when publishing, embedding the content inline. Behavior is now consistent but the published Cargo.toml still contains the relative path, which (a) doesn't resolve on crates.io's web UI for the license link, and (b) makes it hard to audit license provenance from the published manifest alone.

Additionally: any downstream tool parsing our Cargo.toml (cargo-deny, cargo-about, licensee) may flag the relative-path license-file as invalid SPDX, producing false-positive license-compliance errors.

**Severity.** LOW — no operational impact today. Behavior on publish is correct post-cargo-1.77 (the nightly pin per br-1d9y is well past that). Audit-tooling false positives are the main nuisance. Calibrating honestly.

This bead is the LAST truly-in-repo-filable LOW candidate I have. After this, remaining gaps are all GitHub settings (repo topics, Discussions, branch protection) which aren't inspectable from the repo.

**Fix shape — two options:**

1. **Symlinks.** Create `crates/<name>/LICENSE` → `../../LICENSE` symlinks for each crate. Cargo.toml sets `license-file = "LICENSE"` (local). `cargo publish` tarballs the symlinked file. Minimal diff, preserves single-source-of-truth in workspace root.
   - Gotcha: symlinks-to-parents don't survive `git archive` on all platforms; Windows git defaults to copy-on-checkout which breaks the symlink.

2. **Workspace inheritance.** `[workspace.package] license-file = "LICENSE"` (or SPDX `license = "MIT"` if br-dio8 resolves the license-policy question). Each crate does `license-file.workspace = true` or `license.workspace = true`. Cargo resolves the path relative to the workspace root automatically; published tarballs include the correct file.
   - Requires coordination with br-dio8 (license policy) AND br-4clx (workspace.package inheritance as a single pattern).

Recommendation: option 2, bundled with br-4clx when it ships workspace version / crates.io-metadata inheritance. No reason to ship option 1's symlinks transitively.

Blast radius:
- Option 1: 12 new symlinks, 12 Cargo.toml edits (strip the `../../` from license-file paths).
- Option 2: workspace Cargo.toml addition + 12 Cargo.toml edits (replace `license-file = "../../LICENSE"` with `license-file.workspace = true` or `license.workspace = true`).

Zero source changes. Zero test changes.

Prerequisites:
- br-4clx (release workflow) — workspace-inheritance landing point for option 2.
- br-dio8 (license policy) — determines whether SPDX license= or license-file= is the right form.
- br-h8a8 (crates.io metadata) — same workspace.package inheritance wave.

**Related:**
- br-4clx / br-h8a8 / br-dio8 — all three naturally batch with this bead into one "workspace.package inheritance + release metadata polish" PR.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T22:27:50.198309150Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:52:34.360008Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:52:34.360008Z","close_reason":"Already fixed on main via workspace.package inheritance (br-h8a8). Cargo.toml carries license-file = "LICENSE" at workspace root; per-crate Cargo.toml use license-file.workspace = true. No "../../LICENSE" paths remain.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cargo","license","release","review"]}

+            pandas_freq: "B",
+            name: case.name,
+        },
     )
 }
